title: Lycoctonum pseudolaeve
taxonomy: Lycoctonum pseudolaeve is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its leaf and flower morphology. Lycoctonum pseudolaeve is a member of the genus Lycoctonum, which is a small group of plants native to Europe. The Ranunculaceae family consists of over 1,900 species, many of which are found in temperate regions around the world. By understanding its classification, researchers and botanists can better comprehend the evolutionary history and relationships of Lycoctonum pseudolaeve and other related species.
